mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X5 xDrive50e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B58B30S engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, with used units available from £3,000–£4,500. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. Hybrid system re‑commissioning may add extra. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your X5.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X5 xDrive50e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. However, the xDrive50e's PHEV system adds complexity. The main watchpoints are the coolant system (water pump, expansion tank), PCV valve, and hybrid system faults (battery management unit errors, drivetrain malfunctions). With proper maintenance, it's a robust and rewarding powertrain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X5 xDrive50e engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant system failure (water pump, expansion tank), PCV valve failure, and PHEV‑specific drivetrain malfunction / battery management unit errors. High‑voltage battery degradation after 5–7 years or 100,000 km is also a reported concern.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an X5 xDrive50e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. A G05 X5 xDrive50e is a £40,000–£80,000+ vehicle — a £5,000–£6,000 recon is 6–15% of the car's value, making replacement a clear decision. This is well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold. However, high‑voltage battery health significantly affects overall vehicle value.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X5 xDrive50e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The xDrive50e engine code is B58B30S — a 3.0‑litre straight‑six turbo with a 145 kW electric motor. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the common failures on an X5 xDrive50e?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — regular coolant system inspections (water pump, expansion tank), BMW‑spec oil changes with shortened intervals (5,000–7,500 miles), and keeping the hybrid system software up‑to‑date are the most effective prevention. If you see a drivetrain malfunction warning or notice coolant loss, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X5 xDrive50e Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current B58B30S PHEV generation.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X5 xDrive50e engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Hybrid system re-commissioning adds extra time.

cta.label: → Compare X5 xDrive50e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Coolant System Failure (Water Pump / Expansion Tank)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning light, sudden drop in coolant level, overheating, visible coolant puddles, whining noise from the engine bay.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,000 (water pump and thermostat replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant levels drop

problems.recommendation: The B58's electric water pump and expansion tank are known weak points. If you notice the coolant level dropping or hear a whining noise from the engine bay, have the cooling system pressure-tested immediately. Replacing a failing water pump preventatively is far cheaper than the overheating damage it can cause. For xDrive50e owners, the hybrid system adds extra thermal load, making coolant system vigilance even more important.

problems.title: PCV Valve Failure
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, engine misfires, oil in the intake/charge pipe, check engine light, whistling noise from the engine.
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (PCV valve replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: The PCV valve is a known weak point on the B58. If you notice a rough idle, misfires, or oil in the intake, have the PCV system inspected. A failed PCV valve can cause oil consumption and performance issues. Replacing it promptly prevents more serious engine damage.

problems.title: Drivetrain Malfunction / Battery Management Unit Errors (PHEV‑Specific)
problems.symptoms: "Drivetrain malfunction" warning message, inability to start the car, vehicle entering limp mode, sudden loss of power, hybrid system lockout.
problems.repairCost: Varies — often covered under warranty; out‑of‑warranty can be £1,000–£5,000+

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you see a drivetrain malfunction warning, book an inspection immediately

problems.recommendation: The xDrive50e's hybrid system can experience battery management unit (BMU) errors, sometimes caused by the 12V battery or communication faults. Some owners have reported the vehicle being immobilised for extended periods. If you see a drivetrain malfunction warning, do not ignore it — have the hybrid system diagnostics run immediately. Many issues are software‑related and can be resolved with updates, but hardware faults (e.g., battery management unit, high‑voltage battery degradation) require specialist attention. Keep in mind that the xDrive50e's high‑voltage battery may show capacity loss after 5–7 years or 100,000 km.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: X5 xDrive50e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Often software — can be resolved; hardware faults require specialist
Viable on lower‑mileage examples; assess overall vehicle value
Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X5 xDrive50e? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented cooling system maintenance (water pump, expansion tank)
* No coolant loss or temperature fluctuations
* Full BMW service history with correct oil spec
* No history of drivetrain malfunction warnings
avoidIf: * Coolant loss with no diagnosis — walk away
* Rough idle with no PCV history — may indicate PCV failure
* Drivetrain malfunction history with no resolution — potential hybrid system issues
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially complex PHEV issue

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The xDrive50e delivers BMW's most powerful X5 PHEV powertrain — 489 hp from the legendary B58 straight‑six and a 145 kW electric motor. The B58 is a proven, robust engine, but the added hybrid system complexity introduces new watchpoints — coolant system, PCV valve, and battery management unit errors. Buy on full service history, check for software updates, and never ignore a drivetrain malfunction warning. A well‑maintained xDrive50e is a rewarding, long‑lived performance hybrid; a neglected one is a complex and potentially expensive repair.

cta.label: → Compare X5 xDrive50e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X5 xDrive50e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant Level — Check for unexplained loss; water pump or expansion tank may be failing
* Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; B58 is generally quiet
* Rough Idle — Check for uneven running; PCV valve failure indicator
* Drivetrain Warnings — Check for any history of drivetrain malfunction warnings
* Service History — including engine era (B58B30S PHEV) and confirmed maintenance
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved hybrid or engine faults?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
